condition_specific: Brain cancer, bladder cancer, rectal cancer, colon cancer, lymphoma, leukemia, adenocarcinoma, mesothelioma of the respiratory tract
notes: Applies to firefighters and peace officers with identical terms for this cancer list. Must be assigned to hazardous duty for at least 5 years. Must have been exposed to known IARC carcinogen and informed department of exposure. Post-termination: applies to former firefighters/peace officers age 65 or younger diagnosed within 15 years of last employment. Tobacco exclusion applies to respiratory tract cancers only if tobacco is a substantial contributing cause. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence showing specific non-occupational cause.

condition_specific: Brain cancer, bladder cancer, rectal cancer, colon cancer, lymphoma, leukemia, adenocarcinoma, mesothelioma of the respiratory tract
notes: 'Peace officer' means full-time peace officer regularly assigned to hazardous duty as part of special operations, SWAT, EOD, or hazmat response unit. Must be 65 or younger and diagnosed within 15 years of last employment. Tobacco exclusion for respiratory tract cancers. Rebuttable by preponderance.

condition_specific: Buccal cavity and pharynx cancer, esophageal cancer, large intestine cancer, lung cancer, kidney cancer, prostate cancer, skin cancer, stomach cancer, testicular cancer, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, multiple myeloma, malignant melanoma
notes: Firefighter-only expanded cancer list (not available to peace officers). Additional requirement: NFPA 1582-aligned physical exam. Must have been exposed to known IARC carcinogen. Former firefighters age 65 or younger diagnosed within 15 years of last employment. Tobacco exclusion for respiratory tract cancers where tobacco is substantial contributing cause.

condition_specific: Heart-related, perivascular, or pulmonary injury, illness, or death
notes: Event must occur within 24 hours after a known exposure. NFPA 1582-aligned physical exam required. Covers firefighters and volunteer firefighters under § 23-901(6)(d). Tobacco exclusion applies where tobacco is a substantial contributing cause. Rebuttable by preponderance showing specific non-occupational cause.

condition_specific: Heart-related, perivascular, or pulmonary injury, illness, or death
notes: Volunteer firefighters explicitly covered under § 23-901(6)(d) definition. Event must occur within 24 hours after known exposure. NFPA 1582-aligned physical exam required.

condition_specific: Post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD); workers' compensation coverage for PTSD with accepted WC claim
statute_citation: Ariz. Rev. Stat. § 23-972 (conditional on FDA approval of midomafetamine)
notes: Employers shall provide WC coverage for firefighters diagnosed with PTSD by a licensed mental health professional with an accepted WC claim. This is a coverage mandate, not a traditional rebuttable presumption. Section 23-972 conditionally enacted only if FDA approves midomafetamine (MDMA) by December 31, 2025 — FDA did not approve as of the 2026 data date; status uncertain. Separate from the counseling program under § 38-673 (which expressly does not create a WC presumption).

condition_specific: Post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD); workers' compensation coverage for PTSD with accepted WC claim
statute_citation: Ariz. Rev. Stat. § 23-972 (conditional on FDA approval of midomafetamine)
notes: Covers 'certified peace officers' (§ 38-842). Same conditional enactment tied to FDA approval of midomafetamine by December 31, 2025. Coverage mandate with accepted WC claim, not a traditional shifting presumption.
